exemplar.title: Is our most revered knowledge more fragile than we assume it to be? Discuss with reference to the arts and one other area of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: Is our most revered knowledge more fragile than we assume it to be? Discuss with reference to the arts and one other area of knowledge.

strengths: Sustained, clear focus on the prescribed title with precise restatement of the knowledge question and definitions of âreverenceâ and âfragilityâ

Effective use of vivid, specific examples from both the arts (Ai Weiweiâs Dropping a Han Dynasty Urn) and history (Armenian Genocide memory, African soldiers in the World Wars) to illustrate how knowledge can be lost, suppressed, or re-interpreted
Coherent arguments supported by relevant conceptual references to Foucaultâs discourse and Nietzscheâs monumental history, showing an understanding of power dynamics in shaping collective memory
Awareness of alternative perspectives and some evaluation of implications, for instance the paradoxical role of destruction in creating new cultural meaning
weaknesses: The treatment remains largely descriptive, with limited in-depth analysis of how the examples illuminate methods of knowledge construction within each area of knowledge
Counterclaims and alternative views are acknowledged but evaluated only briefly and without explicit criteria, weakening the sophistication of the evaluation
Transitions between sections (particularly from art to history and into the conclusion) lack clear signposting, which occasionally hampers coherence
Engagement with TOK concepts is superficial in placesâunderlying assumptions are invoked but not fully unpacked, and many statements would benefit from deeper critical examination
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The official examinerâs holistic mark of 8 reflects a strong Band 7 performance: the essay sustains a clear, coherent focus on the title, integrates well-chosen examples and conceptual tools, and demonstrates awareness of multiple perspectives. However, it falls short of the highest level because its analysis often remains at a descriptive level, its evaluation of counterclaims is underdeveloped, and some transitions and use of TOK terminology lack depth. On balance, assigning 8 out of 10 accurately captures the essayâs solid strengths in focus, structure and illustrative examples, while acknowledging the areas where deeper critical analysis and clearer evaluation criteria are needed to reach the top band.

annotations.comment: This transitional sentence into the final synthesis would benefit from stronger signposting to clearly indicate the essayâs concluding phase.
The discussion of Ai Weiwei pauses mid-sentence, suggesting an OCR cut; ensure complete presentation of the example for uninterrupted analysis.
Complex, run-on sentences in the introduction reduce clarity; breaking them into concise statements would strengthen coherence and enhance reader comprehension.
Selecting Ai Weiweiâs Dropping a Han Dynasty Urn illustrates the fragility of cultural knowledge with a vivid example, effectively linking art to the knowledge question.
Several factual claims (e.g., one million soldiers) lack citation or specific sources; referencing primary documents would enhance credibility.
The conclusion of this historical section does not explicitly tie back to the knowledge question, missing an opportunity to reinforce the overarching argument.
Introducing Foucaultâs concept of historical discourse effectively highlights powerâs role in shaping collective memory, strengthening the historical analysis.
The transition from art to history lacks a clear signpost; an explicit linking sentence would improve coherence between areas of knowledge.
The student effectively restates the prescribed title in the research question, ensuring clear alignment with the TOK prompt and setting a precise focus for the subsequent discussion.

Definitions of âreverenceâ and âfragilityâ remain broad and lack engagement with TOK concepts such as justification and certainty; specifying these with reference to knowledge frameworks would deepen analysis.
The introduction presents a clear thesis that revered knowledge is fragile yet adaptive, offering a focused roadmap by linking reverence and fragility to the arts and history.
The essay acknowledges the risk that deconstruction of artefacts may sever communitiesâ historical connections, demonstrating awareness of negative consequences.
The student aptly connects Nietzscheâs concept of monumental history to Ai Weiweiâs act, highlighting how uncritical reverence can hinder progress.
While Foucaultâs discourse is cited, the essay does not explore how power relations concretely shape the reinterpretation of art; deeper application of the concept is needed.
The paradox that destroying an artefact can generate new cultural meaning is insightful and demonstrates recognition of fragility as a transformative force.
The footnote formatting diverges from IB standards; ensure consistent citation style in-text to reinforce academic rigour.
Consider introducing a counterexample where preservation of artefacts sustains knowledge, to balance the argument and deepen evaluation of fragility.
The discussion of digital-age information flows is descriptive; illustrating with a recent case study would concretize how fragility operates today.
Analysis remains at the level of description; explicitly evaluating how historical methodologies build resilience would sharpen the argument.
The Armenian Genocide example compellingly demonstrates how political priorities can suppress collective memory, underscoring fragility.
The essay overlooks the criteria by which historians judge when to revive suppressed narratives; specifying these would strengthen evaluative depth.
Highlighting African soldiersâ contributions addresses Eurocentric biases and illustrates how historical fragility can be redressed through renewed scholarship.
The final claim that fragility enables adaptability insightfully captures the essayâs core argument and suggests meaningful implications for knowledge dynamics.
The synthesis effectively parallels art and history, reinforcing how fragility underpins both subjective and evidence-based knowledge.
Introducing the risk of authoritarian manipulation late in the conclusion weakens its impact; earlier integration would allow fuller evaluation.
The conclusion largely restates examples without critiquing underlying assumptions; adding explicit evaluation criteria would elevate the synthesis.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content:
The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
